title: Research scan - constraint extraction for instruction-tuned models
queries_run: formal verification of LLM reasoning steps
key_findings: The strongest direct fit is prompt-side instruction verification: convert instructions into atomic constraints first, then verify the answer against them.
Step-level CoT verification is now technically credible, but only when reasoning traces are reformatted into explicit premises, rules, and typed steps.
Benchmark coverage for fine-grained instruction constraints is finally good enough to evaluate extraction quality directly instead of using answer accuracy as a proxy.
Recent monitorability papers make raw chain-of-thought an unsafe sole source of truth; Carnot needs a fallback path that does not trust CoT by default.

papers.title: Neuro-Symbolic Verification on Instruction Following of LLMs
papers.why_it_matters: Most direct fit to Carnot's current blocker: it treats instruction following as a constraint-satisfaction problem and combines logical plus semantic checks in one verifier.
papers.recommended_carnot_use: Use as the primary template for a prompt-to-constraint intermediate representation plus solver-backed verification path.

papers.title: ConstraintLLM: A Neuro-Symbolic Framework for Industrial-Level Constraint Programming
papers.why_it_matters: Shows that instruction-tuned models can be specialized for constraint programming, paired with retrieval and guided self-correction, and evaluated on an industrial benchmark.
papers.recommended_carnot_use: Borrow the CP modeling pattern for scheduling and resource constraints, especially as a second solver route beside Z3.

papers.title: LLM Self-Correction with DeCRIM: Decompose, Critique, and Refine for Enhanced Following of Instructions with Multiple Constraints
papers.why_it_matters: Directly decomposes instructions into atomic constraints, then critiques failures at the constraint level using RealInstruct and IFEval.
papers.recommended_carnot_use: Build Carnot's first prompt-side atomic constraint extractor and use DeCRIM-style critique labels as supervision.

papers.title: CARE-STaR: Constraint-aware Self-taught Reasoner
papers.why_it_matters: Separates easy versus ambiguous constraints and learns different reasoning traces for different constraint levels.
papers.recommended_carnot_use: Route high-confidence literal constraints to symbolic solvers and keep ambiguous constraints on a softer verification path.

papers.title: VeriCoT: Neuro-symbolic Chain-of-Thought Validation via Logical Consistency Checks
papers.why_it_matters: Formalizes each chain-of-thought step into first-order logic and checks whether each step is grounded in source context, commonsense, or prior steps.
papers.recommended_carnot_use: Prototype a typed step graph for arithmetic and logic traces instead of relying on raw free-form chain-of-thought text.

papers.title: PCRLLM: Proof-Carrying Reasoning with Large Language Models under Stepwise Logical Constraints
papers.why_it_matters: Constrains each reasoning step to explicit premises, rules, and conclusions so chain-level validation becomes possible even for black-box models.
papers.recommended_carnot_use: Adopt the premise-rule-conclusion record format for future step-level verifier experiments.

papers.title: Deductive Verification of Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
papers.why_it_matters: Still the clearest baseline for decomposing verification into small subprocesses with a constrained natural-language reasoning format.
papers.recommended_carnot_use: Use as the baseline to beat for stepwise validation and premise-minimization prompts.

papers.title: Faithful Logical Reasoning via Symbolic Chain-of-Thought
papers.why_it_matters: Translates natural language reasoning into symbolic expressions, then verifies both translation and reasoning with a verifier.
papers.recommended_carnot_use: Good bridge design for a prompt-answer pair where Carnot first converts text to symbolic state and only then verifies.

papers.title: Logic-LM: Empowering Large Language Models with Symbolic Solvers for Faithful Logical Reasoning
papers.why_it_matters: Foundational hybrid pattern: translate to symbolic form, run deterministic inference, and use solver errors to refine the formalization.
papers.recommended_carnot_use: Keep as the minimum viable pattern for solver-backed extraction experiments.

papers.title: Typed Chain-of-Thought: A Curry-Howard Framework for Verifying LLM Reasoning
papers.why_it_matters: Provides a formal lens for mapping informal chain-of-thought into typed proof objects and treating well-typedness as a certificate of faithfulness.
papers.recommended_carnot_use: Use as design guidance for a typed intermediate representation rather than free-form regex over reasoning text.

benchmark_assets.title: FollowBench: A Multi-level Fine-grained Constraints Following Benchmark for Large Language Models
benchmark_assets.use_for_carnot: Seed a prompt-side benchmark with explicit content, situation, style, format, and example constraints.

benchmark_assets.title: CFBench: A Comprehensive Constraints-Following Benchmark for LLMs
benchmark_assets.use_for_carnot: Adds broader real-life constraint taxonomies and requirement-priority scoring.

benchmark_assets.title: RealInstruct
benchmark_assets.use_for_carnot: Real user multi-constraint instructions are a better supervision source than synthetic prompt lists.

benchmark_assets.title: VIFBench
benchmark_assets.use_for_carnot: Instruction-following verifier benchmark with fine-grained labels; closest external evaluation target to Carnot's gap.

benchmark_assets.title: IndusCP
benchmark_assets.use_for_carnot: Industrial constraint-programming tasks for scheduling and resource-allocation extraction.

benchmark_assets.title: P-FOLIO
benchmark_assets.use_for_carnot: Human-written stepwise logical proofs for evaluating step-level reasoning extraction.

benchmark_assets.title: FormalBench
benchmark_assets.use_for_carnot: Program-semantics benchmark where formal specification inference is the task itself.

benchmark_assets.title: StructFlowBench
benchmark_assets.use_for_carnot: Useful if Carnot extends from single-turn extraction to multi-turn structural constraints.

risk_evidence.title: Monitoring Reasoning Models for Misbehavior and the Risks of Promoting Obfuscation
risk_evidence.why_it_matters: Shows chain-of-thought can help oversight, but strong optimization pressure can induce obfuscated reasoning.

risk_evidence.title: Can Reasoning Models Obfuscate Reasoning? Stress-Testing Chain-of-Thought Monitorability
risk_evidence.why_it_matters: Explicit stress test showing some models can hide adversarial intent under obfuscation pressure.

risk_evidence.title: Measuring Chain-of-Thought Monitorability Through Faithfulness and Verbosity
risk_evidence.why_it_matters: Useful metric design: faithful traces can still be poor monitors when they omit crucial factors.

risk_evidence.title: Diagnosing Pathological Chain-of-Thought in Reasoning Models
risk_evidence.why_it_matters: Gives concrete pathology categories and cheap diagnostics for post-hoc rationalization, encoded reasoning, and internalized reasoning.

risk_evidence.title: Lie to Me: How Faithful Is Chain-of-Thought Reasoning in Reasoning Models?
risk_evidence.why_it_matters: Recent cross-model evidence that faithfulness varies sharply by family and hint type, which argues for model-specific monitorability gates.

proposed_experiments_2026_04_15.title: Instruction-to-Constraint IR Benchmark
proposed_experiments_2026_04_15.goal: Build a gold benchmark of atomic prompt constraints from FollowBench, RealInstruct, CFBench, and VIFBench, then measure extraction recall and false positives on instruction-tuned models.
proposed_experiments_2026_04_15.hypothesis: Prompt-side decomposition will reduce false positives more than answer-only regex extraction because the verifier will know exactly which constraints matter before inspecting the response.
proposed_experiments_2026_04_15.success_criteria: Atomic constraint recall >= 0.85 on the curated benchmark, satisfied-constraint false-positive rate <= 0.05, and measurable improvement over the current regex plus Z3 promptless path.

proposed_experiments_2026_04_15.title: Dual-Path CoT Verifier with Typed Step Graphs
proposed_experiments_2026_04_15.goal: Implement a step-level verifier for arithmetic and logic traces using premise-rule-conclusion records inspired by VeriCoT, PCRLLM, Deductive Verification, and Typed CoT.
proposed_experiments_2026_04_15.hypothesis: A typed step graph will catch errors that answer-only checking misses, but only when combined with prompt-derived constraints and a fallback to answer-level verification.
proposed_experiments_2026_04_15.success_criteria: On a live instruction-tuned cohort, catch >= 25% of wrong answers missed by prompt-only verification while adding < 2% extra false positives on correct answers.

proposed_experiments_2026_04_15.title: CoT Monitorability Audit and Fallback Policy
proposed_experiments_2026_04_15.goal: Measure whether Qwen and Gemma instruction-tuned models expose enough faithful reasoning to justify CoT-based extraction, using recent faithfulness and pathology metrics.
proposed_experiments_2026_04_15.hypothesis: Monitorability differs by model family and task, so Carnot should gate CoT extraction behind a measured trust score rather than assuming traces are faithful.
proposed_experiments_2026_04_15.success_criteria: Produce a per-model monitorability score, a pathology breakdown, and a simple policy that predicts when to trust CoT extraction versus prompt-answer-only verification.

recommendation_summary.primary_direction: Build a prompt-to-constraint intermediate representation before attempting richer answer verification.
recommendation_summary.secondary_direction: Treat CoT as optional evidence gated by measured monitorability, not as Carnot's only extraction source.
